name: DPDP (India personal data)
description: Guards for Indian personal data under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act: Aadhaar and PAN literals, personal data in logs and URLs. Evidence for DPDP diligence — evidence, not certification.

policies.name: no-aadhaar-literals
policies.rule: Aadhaar numbers must never appear as literals in source code or fixtures.
policies.skill: Use clearly-invalid placeholders (Aadhaar never starts with 0 or 1 — use 1111 1111 1111 in fixtures) or generated fakes.
policies.fixSuggestion: Remove the Aadhaar number; use a clearly-invalid placeholder starting with 0 or 1.

policies.name: no-pan-card-literals
policies.rule: Indian PAN (tax) numbers must never appear as literals in source code or fixtures.
policies.skill: PAN format is AAAAA9999A. Use an invalid placeholder like AAAAA0000A in tests.
policies.fixSuggestion: Replace with an invalid-format placeholder PAN.

policies.name: no-personal-data-in-logs
policies.rule: Personal identifiers (Aadhaar, PAN, phone, email, address) must not be written to logs.
policies.skill: Log opaque user IDs. Personal data in logs creates an uncontrolled copy the DPDP fiduciary duties still apply to.
policies.fixSuggestion: Log the internal user UUID instead of personal identifiers.

policies.name: no-pii-in-urls
policies.rule: Personal identifiers must not be passed in URLs or query strings.
policies.skill: Send personal data in request bodies over HTTPS; use opaque identifiers in paths.
policies.fixSuggestion: Move the identifier to the request body or use an opaque UUID path parameter.
